name: CSS Inserter

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Changed html in the **Settings** panel to remove unnecessary nested `<span>`'s.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Erallie/css-inserter/compare/1.0.14...1.0.15

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Made the submenu compatible with mobile *(hopefully)* when you insert CSS snippets from the context menu.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Erallie/css-inserter/compare/1.0.13...1.0.14

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Grouped the all css snippets into a submenu in the context menu.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Erallie/css-inserter/compare/1.0.11...1.0.13

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:

- Removed unnecessary `executeCommandById()`

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Erallie/css-inserter/compare/1.0.10...1.0.11

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fixed an issue where renaming a newly-created CSS Snippet creates an extra command.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Updated the License, the manifest, and settings.ts to implement requested changes by the [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/) review team.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Just a re-release since I changed something and changed it back.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
In this version I:

 - Updated the README.md to be more readable and fixed a heading that I missed to follow the Obsidian [Style guide](https://help.obsidian.md/Contributing+to+Obsidian/Style+guide).
 - Changed the CSS to be more compatible with mobile.
 - Added icons to the commands themselves for mobile compatibility.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This is tested more thoroughly and is ready for review.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: I previously released this because I thought it was ready to be submitted to obsidian, but now I have a way to test it out on other platforms, so I will hold off.

releases.description: Added a readme and made "class" and "style" input fields optional.
